items.title: sdmxflow
items.text:
Version: 0.1.0

sdmxflow downloads SDMX datasets into a reproducible, append-only on-disk layout designed for data warehouse and periodic refresh workflows.

items.title: Status and provider support
items.text:
• Status: early but functional
  • Provider support: Eurostat (source_id="ESTAT")

items.title: What you get
items.text:
Given an output folder, sdmxflow writes:

  • dataset.csv — append-only facts table
  • metadata.json — version history and operational metadata
  • codelists/ — exported reference tables needed to interpret coded columns

items.title: Quickstart
items.text:
from pathlib import Path

from sdmxflow.dataset import SdmxDataset

ds = SdmxDataset(
    out_dir=Path("./out/lfsa_egai2d"),
    source_id="ESTAT",
    dataset_id="lfsa_egai2d",
    # Optional:
    # agency_id="ESTAT",
    # key=None,
    # params={...},
    save_logs=True,  # writes <out_dir>/logs/<agency>__<dataset>__<timestamp>.log
)

result = ds.fetch()
print("Appended new version:", result.appended)
print("Dataset CSV:", result.dataset_csv)
print("Metadata JSON:", result.metadata_json)
print("Codelists dir:", result.codelists_dir)

Next:

  • See Getting started for installation and usage patterns.
  • See Output layout for the artifact contract.
  • See Logging for the INFO-level contract and per-run log files.

items.title: API
items.text: This documentation covers the user-facing API shipped in version 0.1.0.

items.title: SdmxDataset
items.text:
Create a downloader bound to one dataset and one output folder.

Constructor parameters:

  • out_dir (required): root folder for artifacts
  • source_id (required): provider id (currently only "ESTAT")
  • dataset_id (required): dataset id within the provider
  • agency_id (optional): defaults to source_id for Eurostat
  • key (optional): provider-specific SDMX key restriction (string or mapping)
  • params (optional): provider-specific passthrough query parameters
  • logger (optional): custom logging.Logger
  • save_logs (optional): write a per-run log file under <out_dir>/logs/

Common usage:

from sdmxflow.dataset import SdmxDataset

ds = SdmxDataset(out_dir="./out", source_id="ESTAT", dataset_id="lfsa_egai2d")
result = ds.fetch()

items.title: fetch()
items.text:
Runs a single refresh cycle:

  • checks upstream "last updated",
  • compares to local metadata,
  • downloads/appends if changed,
  • updates metadata and codelists.

Returns a FetchResult.

items.title: FetchResult
items.text:
FetchResult is a small value object that points to written artifacts:

  • out_dir
  • dataset_csv
  • metadata_json
  • codelists_dir
  • appended (bool)

items.title: Credits
items.text:
• The early direction for warehouse-friendly SDMX refresh workflows was influenced by a dlt SDMX extension gist shared by Martin Salo.
  • Core SDMX protocol/model handling is powered by the sdmx1 Python package.

items.title: Documentation tooling
items.text:
This documentation site is built with Zensical:

  • Project: https://zensical.org/

items.title: Setup
items.text:
This project uses uv for development.

uv sync --group dev

items.title: Tests
items.text: uv run pytest

items.title: Lint / format
items.text:
uv run ruff check .
uv run ruff format .

items.title: Build / preview docs locally
items.text:
Install docs dependencies (included in the dev group) and run:

uv run zensical serve

Zensical will print a local URL (usually http://localhost:8000/).

items.title: Upgrade Zensical
items.text:
Zensical is a dev dependency. To upgrade it with uv:

  1. Update the zensical version spec in pyproject.toml (both the [project.optional-dependencies].dev and [dependency-groups].dev lists are currently present in this project).
  2. Re-sync the environment:
uv sync --group dev

To show the currently installed version:

uv pip show zensical

items.title: Release to PyPI
items.text:
Publishing to PyPI is automated via GitHub Actions on version tags matching v* (e.g. v0.1.0). The workflow validates that the tag version matches pyproject.toml before building and publishing.

Prerequisite: configure PyPI "Trusted Publishing" (OIDC) for the GitHub repository so the pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish step can upload without storing an API token in GitHub secrets.

items.title: CLI demo (live network)
items.text:
If you want a runnable, end-to-end example (good for trying sdmxflow the first time or referencing from blog posts / README snippets), use the script in:

  • scripts/download_dataset.py

This script performs live network I/O against the provider and writes a stable on-disk artifact layout (dataset CSV + metadata + codelists).

items.title: Run from this repository
items.text:
uv sync --group dev
uv run python scripts/download_dataset.py --help

Download the default Eurostat example dataset into ./out/estat/lfsa_egai2d:

uv run python scripts/download_dataset.py

Write a per-run log file under <out_dir>/logs/ (useful for debugging provider behavior):

uv run python scripts/download_dataset.py --save-logs

Pick an explicit output folder and increase verbosity:

uv run python scripts/download_dataset.py \
  --out-dir ./out/lfsa_egai2d \
  --log-level DEBUG \
  --save-logs

items.title: What to expect
items.text:
• Console logs at the level you chose via --log-level.
  • Output artifacts under your --out-dir (see Output layout).

Next:

  • See Output layout for the artifact contract.
  • See Logging for the library's INFO-level logging contract.

items.title: From PyPI
items.text:
Once published:

uv pip install sdmxflow

If you want to manage sdmxflow as part of a Python project, you can also add it to your project's dependencies with uv:

uv add sdmxflow

items.title: From source (this repository)
items.text:
This project uses uv for development.

git clone https://github.com/knifflig/sdmxflow
cd sdmxflow
uv sync --group dev

items.title: Documentation site (Zensical)
items.text:
This repository uses Zensical to render the docs.

Preview locally:

uv run zensical serve

Build the static site:

uv run zensical build --clean

items.title: Basic usage
items.text:
The main entrypoint is SdmxDataset.

from pathlib import Path

from sdmxflow.dataset import SdmxDataset

ds = SdmxDataset(
    out_dir=Path("./out/my_dataset"),
    source_id="ESTAT",
    dataset_id="lfsa_egai2d",
)

result = ds.fetch()
print(result.appended)

items.title: Refresh behavior
items.text:
fetch() is designed for scheduled refresh jobs:

  1. Query the provider for the dataset's upstream "last updated" timestamp.
  2. Compare it to the latest recorded version in metadata.json.
  3. If unchanged, skip download.
  4. If changed, download and append a new slice to dataset.csv, then update metadata and codelists.

items.title: Parameters you will commonly use
items.text:
• out_dir: the dataset artifact folder
  • source_id: currently only "ESTAT"
  • dataset_id: Eurostat dataset id (e.g. "lfsa_egai2d")
  • save_logs=True: write a per-run debug log file under <out_dir>/logs/

For the full parameter list, see API.

items.title: Runnable example script
items.text: For an end-to-end example you can run immediately (including logging and output artifacts), see Examples.

items.title: Logging
items.text: sdmxflow uses the standard library logging module and does not configure handlers.

items.title: INFO-level contract
items.text:
At INFO level, SdmxDataset.fetch() emits exactly three user-facing messages per call:

  1. Fetch intention (what will be fetched and where)
  2. Version decision (download vs already up to date)
  3. Completion summary (artifact paths)

All other detail is emitted at DEBUG.

items.title: Enabling logs
items.text:
In applications, configure logging once at process startup:

import logging

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)

For troubleshooting:

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

items.title: Per-run log files
items.text:
If you pass save_logs=True to SdmxDataset, a per-run log file is written under:

  • <out_dir>/logs/<agency>__<dataset>__<timestamp>.log

The file handler is attached only for the duration of fetch() and is detached/closed in a finally block.

items.title: Output layout
items.text:
sdmxflow writes a stable, warehouse-friendly folder structure under your chosen out_dir:

<out_dir>/
  dataset.csv
  metadata.json
  codelists/
    ... reference CSVs ...
  logs/                     # only when save_logs=True
    <agency>__<dataset>__<timestamp>.log

items.title: dataset.csv
items.text:
• Append-only across upstream versions.
  • Includes a leading last_updated column (UTC ISO-8601) indicating which upstream version each row belongs to.

This makes it easy to:

  • reprocess from scratch (read the full file), or
  • process incrementally (filter by last_updated).

items.title: metadata.json
items.text:
metadata.json stores operational metadata and version history, such as:

  • upstream timestamps,
  • fetch times,
  • URL / HTTP status (when available),
  • number of appended rows per version.

The schema is owned by sdmxflow and is intended to be compact and stable.

items.title: codelists/
items.text:
The codelists/ directory contains exported reference data needed to interpret coded dataset columns (dimensions/attributes).

Codelists tend to change less frequently than facts, but fetch() ensures you have the required reference tables alongside the dataset.
